name: Goat Funded Trader
description_short: Goat Funded Trader is a prop firm with 6+ challenge models—including instant funding and a $1 entry option—offering up to 100% profit splits and $2M in simulated capital.
description_long:
## Why the $1 Challenge changes the entry-cost calculus

Goat Funded Trader is one of the few prop firms letting you test the funnel for the price of a coffee. The $1 Challenge is not a gimmick — it is a real evaluation that, if passed, converts to a simulated funded account with an 80% profit split. That single design choice is the most important thing to understand about Goat Funded Trader before comparing it to FTMO or Apex. Most prop firms in this category charge $50-$200 minimum to even see the platform. Goat Funded Trader lets you test their execution, dashboard, and payout flow for a dollar before committing to a real challenge fee.

The trade-off: a $1 challenge is not going to fund a career. Passing it gets you a small simulated account. It is a proof-of-concept for the firm, not a path to income. Use it to decide if you want to buy a real 2-Step challenge, not as your primary funding strategy.

## The six-model maze and which one you actually want

Goat Funded Trader runs 1-Step, 2-Step, 3-Step, Instant, Goat Blitz, and the $1 Challenge — plus separate Standard, PRO, and GOAT model variants inside those. This is more optionality than most traders need and it makes side-by-side comparison harder than it should be.

The practical breakdown: the **2-Step is the mainstream product**, priced from **$26 for a $5K account, $119 for $25K, and $499 for $100K**. Two evaluation phases, no time limit, 3 minimum trading days, 80% profit split scaling to 100%. This is the plan most reviews and community threads reference.

The **Instant Goat $100K at $899** skips evaluation entirely — you pay more upfront and get immediate simulated capital with 10-day payout cycles. It looks appealing until you notice there is no refund on failure, so the effective cost of a blown Instant account is the full $899 versus recovering a much smaller sunk cost on a failed 2-Step.

**Goat Blitz** and the 1-Step and 3-Step models exist for traders who specifically want faster or slower evaluation cadence. If you do not already know which one fits your style, the 2-Step is the default answer.

## What the simulated-capital model actually means

Goat Funded Trader operates on the standard prop-firm structure: you trade a demo account, the firm mirrors or does not mirror your positions on the live side, and you get paid a share of the simulated profits as a real cash reward. The marketing line "You're not liable for any losses" is accurate — this is not a leveraged personal account, and you are not signing a broker agreement.

What this means in practice: your rewards are contractual payouts from Goat Funded Trader, not a share of real market P&L. If the firm has a bad quarter or changes its terms, your "funding" evaporates. This is true of every prop firm in this category, but it deserves stating because the language on the site frames rewards as if they were profits from your trading. They are payouts from a private company based on your simulated performance.

## The Stage 4 scaling math (getting to $2M)

The headline number — **up to $2M simulated capital and 100% profit split at Stage 4** — requires reaching the top of Goat Funded Trader's 4-stage scaling plan. This is a real feature, and it is meaningful for consistent traders, but it is not the account you start on.

Every scaling plan in prop trading gates the top tier behind sustained profitability over months. Expect to hit consistency requirements, drawdown ceilings, and profit-target milestones at each stage. The $2M number is a marketing anchor, not the account size you should plan around when deciding whether Goat Funded Trader fits your goals. If your realistic ceiling is a $100K or $200K funded account, budget your fee accordingly — do not buy the biggest available challenge assuming you will scale to Stage 4 quickly.

The **100% profit split** at the top stage is genuinely competitive. Most established firms cap at 80-90% even for veteran traders.

## The news trading cap and other rule fine print

Goat Funded Trader allows news trading, weekend holding, and unlimited trading periods — three things that older firms restrict. However, **news trading profit is capped at 1% per trade**, which effectively kills event-driven strategies that rely on outsized NFP or CPI moves. If your edge is macro releases, this cap will neutralize most of the benefit of "news trading allowed."

Other rules worth noting from the promo material: **10% profit target per phase, 4% maximum daily loss, 6% maximum overall loss, and leverage up to 1:100**. These are close to industry standard. The 4% daily loss ceiling is on the tighter side compared to FTMO's 5% but looser than some newer firms at 3%.

Payouts are advertised at every 14 days for standard accounts and every 10 days for Instant. The site's "Reward Guarantee" promises an extra $1,000 if payout does not process within a stated window — a policy worth screenshotting when you sign up, since terms on prop firm sites change frequently.

## Mobile execution is not what the site implies

The Goat Funded Trader mobile app handles analytics and account monitoring only. **Trade execution requires MT5, Match-Trader, or TradeLocker on desktop**. If you were expecting to place and manage funded trades from your phone, you will need a separate mobile app from one of those platforms. This is a common gap across prop firms, but worth calling out because the marketing implies a fuller mobile experience than exists.

For traders who scalp or manage tight intraday risk, this desktop dependency is not a dealbreaker — most professional workflows run on MT5 anyway. For part-time traders hoping to check and adjust positions from a phone during work hours, factor in the third-party mobile app you will need.

## Where Goat Funded Trader sits against the established firms

Goat Funded Trader was **founded in 2023** by Wishes Tower International Limited, based in Hong Kong. The firm has paid out over $23M in rewards to a claimed 250,000+ traders — real numbers, but shorter track record than FTMO (2015), Topstep (2012), or Apex Trader Funding (2021). If firm longevity is a signal you weight heavily, this is a legitimate concern. Prop firms have failed and disappeared with trader payouts owed, and a 2023 firm has not yet weathered a full market cycle.

Against FTMO, Goat Funded Trader is cheaper at entry, more flexible on rules, and offers a wider asset selection — 500+ crypto CFDs versus FTMO's narrower crypto list. FTMO has a longer track record and a more established payout reputation.

Against Apex Trader Funding and Topstep, Goat Funded Trader competes on forex and CFDs rather than futures. If futures are your market, Apex or Topstep is the more direct fit. Goat Funded Trader has recently added futures product tabs, but this is not their primary strength.

## Who should look elsewhere

Skip Goat Funded Trader if: you trade primarily US futures (Apex or Topstep is purpose-built for that), you rely on high-impact news events for edge (the 1% news cap will hurt), you need mobile trade execution as a core workflow, or you specifically want a firm with 5+ years of payout history.

Goat Funded Trader makes sense for forex and multi-asset traders who want low-friction entry, a wide range of challenge models to test, crypto CFD access, and the option to scale to a 100% profit split. The $1 Challenge is the strongest recruitment mechanism in the current prop firm market, and if you have been on the fence about testing this category, it removes the price objection almost entirely.

tiers.name: Goat 2-Step $100K
features: 100% profit split and on-demand payouts at Stage 4

pros: $1 Challenge entry removes the standard prop firm price barrier
Six challenge models including 1-Step, 2-Step, 3-Step, Instant, and Goat Blitz
Up to $2M simulated capital and 100% profit split at Stage 4 scaling
500+ crypto CFDs, wider than most forex-focused prop firms
News trading, weekend holding, and no time limit on most evaluations
Frequent 50% off promo codes and crypto payment options
cons: Founded 2023 with shorter payout track record than FTMO, Topstep, or Apex
News trading profit capped at 1% per trade, neutralizing event-driven edge
Mobile app is analytics only; execution requires MT5, Match-Trader, or TradeLocker desktop
Instant Goat challenge fees are non-refundable if the account is blown
Six overlapping model variants make direct plan comparison harder than it should be
best_for: Traders wanting to test a prop firm funnel at minimal cost
Crypto CFD traders needing broad asset selection
Consistent traders targeting the 100% profit split scaling tier
Budget-conscious traders priced out of FTMO-level entry fees

company_background: Goat Funded Trader is operated by Wishes Tower International Limited, headquartered in Hong Kong. The firm was founded in 2023 and has grown quickly to a claimed 250,000+ traders worldwide with over $23M in reported reward payouts. It positions itself in the forex and CFD prop firm category with recent expansion into futures products.
